pages.title: Physiotherapy and Massage in Sofia | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Physiotherapy and massage in Sofia for back pain, neck pain, shoulder pain, tingling, posture problems and muscle imbalance. Functional assessment and an individual movement plan at DM Physio.
pages.text: Physiotherapy and Massage in Sofia | DM Physio Physiotherapy and massage in Sofia for back pain, neck pain, shoulder pain, tingling, posture problems and muscle imbalance. Functional assessment and an individual movement plan at DM Physio. Professional physiotherapy in Sofia DM Physio Pain keeps coming back? Start with assessment, not guessing. We look at movement, posture, muscle control and daily habits to understand what may be keeping pain, stiffness or tingling active. See how we work Book an appointment 8000+ patient visits 95% Success rate 7+ years of clinical experience Why are you here? Do you have pain , tingling or the feeling that your body is compensating because of a muscle imbalance ? Have you tried rest, medication, massage or exercises, but the symptoms keep coming back ? That usually means the painful area may be only one part of the problem. The next step is to check how you move, load your body and control the muscles around the problem. Physiotherapy in Sofia: how we work at DM Physio We do not ask you to choose a procedure blindly. We begin with a functional assessment of posture, movement, strength, muscle control and daily habits, so the therapy plan is based on what your body actually shows. Activating underused muscles Pain is often influenced not only by what is overloaded, but also by what is not contributing enough . We check which muscles need better activation and control, then build them back into movement. Releasing overloaded areas When muscles stay tense or shortened, they can change how joints move and increase load in nearby areas. We identify these patterns and use manual techniques, movement and exercises to restore better balance. Understanding the mechanism Progress is easier when you understand why symptoms return. We explain which positions, habits or loading patterns may keep the problem active and how to adjust them. At DM Physio , we do not focus only on the symptom - we look for the factors that provoke and maintain it. Start with a clear assessment, not another guess Book an appointment What the recovery process looks like Everyday stress on the body - long sitting, repetitive movement, posture, training load or work positions - can gradually create muscle imbalance, stiffness and recurring tension. The goal is to understand these factors and build a plan that fits your body. 🩺 Functional assessment We assess the whole body because pain in one area can be influenced by another. We look at posture, movement and habits to understand what may be contributing to the symptoms. 🧩 Clear explanation We use functional tests and explain the findings in plain language, so you understand what may be provoking the pain and what can be changed. 📋 Individual plan We build a personal plan with the most suitable therapy, exercises and home advice for your condition, goals and response. 🤝 Ongoing support We follow your progress and adapt the plan when needed, so you are not left guessing what to do next. Common conditions Temple pain Headache and temple tension can be related to muscle overload, posture and sensitivity around the head and neck. Cervical disc herniation A cervical disc problem may be linked with neck pain, arm pain, tingling or changes in how the arm feels. Piriformis syndrome Piriformis-related irritation can refer pain or tingling through the buttock and down the leg. Lower back pain Lower back pain can involve several factors: posture, load, muscle control, mobility and daily habits. What patients say about us Reviews from patients Where is the pain? The clearer the cause becomes, the easier it is to build a useful plan. pages.title: Anti-cellulite massage — step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Anti-cellulite massage to stimulate blood circulation and lymphatic drainage, breakdown of fat deposits and smoother, tighter skin. Techniques: ergon, cupping, kneading, squeezing and rubbing.
pages.text: Anti-cellulite massage — step by step | DM Physio Anti-cellulite massage to stimulate blood circulation and lymphatic drainage, breakdown of fat deposits and smoother, tighter skin. Techniques: ergon, cupping, kneading, squeezing and rubbing. Anti-cellulite massage - step by step Anti-cellulite Massage step by step Anti-cellulite massage is a specialized technique aimed at improving the appearance of skin affected by cellulite. Through a combination of firmer manual techniques, the session aims to support circulation, lymphatic drainage and the appearance of the skin. Procedure (60 min.) Anti-cellulite massage includes specific manual techniques such as ergon, cupping, kneading, squeezing and rubbing, which are intense and targeted to the areas affected by cellulite (thighs, buttocks). These moves have several key effects: Stimulation of blood circulation - supports local tissue circulation. Improving lymphatic drainage — removes toxins and excess fluid. Work on dense tissue areas - may improve the feel and appearance of the skin. Skin tightening and smoothing — improves elasticity and texture. Swelling reduction — reduces water retention. For a more noticeable effect, a series of procedures is often recommended, often combined with specialized products (oils, creams) and a healthy lifestyle — balanced nutrition and regular physical activity. Price: 50 euros. (60min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently asked questions about Anti-Cellulite Massage How many treatments are needed to see results? Many people choose a course of 10-15 procedures, 1-2 times a week, for a more noticeable effect. Maintenance procedures are done once a month or every two months. Is the anti-cellulite massage painful? It is more intense than relaxing and can cause acceptable discomfort, especially at the beginning or in areas with more pronounced cellulite. The pressure is always adjusted to your tolerance and usually the discomfort decreases with the sessions. Are there any side effects after the massage? Slight redness is possible, and with more pronounced cellulite and bruises on the treated areas. This is temporary. The next procedure is done after their complete disappearance. Drink enough water and hydrate the skin. Can I lose weight with anti-cellulite massage? Massage does not replace nutrition, movement or medical advice for weight loss. It may support skin texture and reduce puffiness when combined with healthy habits. Pain conditions

pages.title: Cupping massage — step by step DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Cupping massage (cupping therapy) with vacuum to stimulate circulation, relax muscles and fascia and aid in detoxification. Suitable for pain, inflammation and tension.
pages.text: Cupping massage — step by step DM Physio Cupping massage (cupping therapy) with vacuum to stimulate circulation, relax muscles and fascia and aid in detoxification. Suitable for pain, inflammation and tension. Cupping massage — step by step Cupping Massage step by step Cupping massage, also known as cupping therapy, is an ancient recovery practice that uses special cups (cups) placed on the skin to create a vacuum. This vacuum sucks the skin and underlying tissues upward, stimulating circulation, releasing tension in muscles and fascia, and aiding detoxification. This is an extremely effective method for relieving pain, reducing inflammation and improving the general condition of the tissues. Procedure (60 min.) During the cupping massage, the therapist carefully places the cups on the problem areas, creating a vacuum. Cups can be left in place for a few minutes (dry cupping) or moved over the skin after applying oil (dynamic/sliding cupping). The main benefits of cupping massage include: Improved circulation: Increases the flow of oxygen and nutrients to the tissues. Reducing muscle tension: Relieves cramps, knots and spasms. Detoxification: Helps remove toxins and metabolic waste from the body. Pain and inflammation relief: Effective for chronic pain in the back, neck, shoulders and other areas. Cupping massage is a powerful alternative therapy that offers short-term relief and promotes overall body health and recovery. Before the Massage (5-10 min.) Brief Consultation and Assessment Before the cupping massage begins, the therapist will conduct a brief consultation to understand your specific symptoms, areas of pain or tension, and any medical history that may affect the session. This assessment is important to determine if cupping therapy is right for you and to focus on the areas most in need. Identification of treatment areas Discussion of contraindications (if any) Tailoring therapy for maximum comfort and results Learn more about assessment Price: 50 euros. Book an appointment Frequently asked questions about Cupping Massage: What is cupping massage? Cupping massage (cupping therapy) is a method that uses glass or silicone cups that are placed on the skin. By creating a vacuum, the skin and underlying tissues are suctioned, which stimulates blood circulation, releases muscle tension and helps detoxify the body. Is cupping massage painful and will it leave marks? During the cupping massage, a slight stimulation is felt - a slight pulling or pressure on the tissues, but it should not cause severe pain. If red or purple circles appear, it means that small capillaries have broken and blood has clotted in the subcutaneous tissue - an effect that is not targeted and is not a sign of properly conducted therapy. With correct technique, suction cups stimulate circulation without causing permanent bruising; any residual stains indicate a too long or aggressive application and can be avoided by optimal vacuum and dwell time. How often can cupping massage be done? Frequency depends on your condition and goals of therapy. To relieve chronic pain, it can be done once or twice a week. For general well-being and detoxification, it can be done less often. Your therapist will give you specific recommendations after the initial consultation. Are there any contraindications for cupping massage? Yes, there are some contraindications, such as: open wounds, severely inflamed skin, varicose veins in the treated area, hemophilia, serious heart disease or pregnancy (in some areas). Always inform the therapist of your medical condition to ensure that the session is safe for you. Pain conditions

pages.title: Deep tissue massage - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Deep tissue massage for chronic pain, muscle knots (trigger points), stiffness and limited mobility. We reach the deeper layers of muscle and fascia for long-lasting relief.
pages.text: Deep tissue massage - step by step | DM Physio Deep tissue massage for chronic pain, muscle knots (trigger points), stiffness and limited mobility. We reach the deeper layers of muscle and fascia for long-lasting relief. Deep tissue massage - step by step Deep Tissue Massage step by step Deep tissue massage is a therapeutic technique aimed at reaching the deeper layers of muscle and connective tissue. It is particularly effective in relieving chronic pain, muscle knots ("trigger points"), limited mobility and stiffness, which often result from poor posture, repetitive motion or old injuries. Procedure (90 min.) In deep tissue massage, the therapist uses slow but intense movements with more pressure to affect the muscle fibers, fascia and other connective tissues. The goal is to break up adhesions, loosen deep knots, and restore normal tissue function. This type of massage is extremely useful for: Relief of chronic pain — especially in the neck, back and shoulders. Deactivation of trigger points that cause referred pain. Improving posture by releasing tight muscles. Increasing range of motion and restoring tissue elasticity. Sports injury recovery — reduces inflammation and promotes recovery. Although you may feel discomfort at certain times, the sensation should be "therapeutic pain" — tolerable and leading to tangible relief after the session. Regular procedures give a cumulative effect: easier movement, less tension and better daily tone. Price: 65 euros. (90min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently Asked Questions about Deep Tissue Massage When is deep tissue massage appropriate? It is suitable for chronic pain, muscle knots ("trigger points"), stiffness, limited mobility and in recovery phases after overuse or sports injuries. It is also beneficial for athletes and active exercisers. Is deep tissue massage painful? Due to the depth of the pressure, there may be moments of "good pain", especially in tense areas. This is expected and indicates that we are reaching the problem tissues. The pressure always adapts to remain tolerable and effective. How often should it be done? It depends on the condition. We often start with 1-2 sessions per week for severe symptoms, then switch to a maintenance regimen every 2-3 weeks. The therapist will suggest a personalized schedule based on tissue response and your goals. Are there any side effects after the massage? Mild muscle soreness is possible for 24–48 hours and sometimes minor bruising in areas of high stress—a feeling similar to that after an intense workout. Hydration, gentle movement and gentle stretching help speed recovery. Pain conditions

pages.excerpt: Deep tissue massage for chronic pain, trigger points, stiffness and limited mobility. It reaches the deeper layers of muscles and fascia for long-lasting relief.

Procedure (60 min.) Slow but intense movements are used with more pressure to affect muscle fibers, fascia and other connective tissues. Sports injury recovery — reduces inflammation and promotes recovery. There may be momentary discomfort ("therapeutic pain"), but it should be tolerable and lead to relief after the session. Before the Massage (5–10 min.) Brief Consultation and Assessment Before the start, we conduct a short consultation: symptoms, pain areas, medical history. This allows priority areas to be identified and techniques to be adapted for maximum efficiency and safety. Precise determination of problem areas An individual approach to your condition Optimal results Learn more about assessment Price: 50 euros. (60min) Book an appointment Frequently Asked Questions about Deep Tissue Massage When is deep tissue massage appropriate? For chronic pain, muscle knots ("trigger points"), stiffness, limited mobility and in recovery phases after overuse or sports injuries. It is also suitable for active exercisers. Is deep tissue massage painful? A "good ache" is possible, especially in tense areas — a sign that we are reaching the problem tissues. Pressure adapts to remain tolerable and effective. How often should it be done? It is often started with 1–2 sessions per week for severe symptoms, then switched to a maintenance regimen every 2–3 weeks according to individual response. Are there any side effects after the massage? Mild muscle soreness for 24–48 hours and sometimes minor bruising in high-stress areas — similar to a feeling after an intense workout — are possible. Hydration and light exercise speed up recovery. Pain conditions

pages.title: Dry needling therapy — step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: How dry needling works at DM Physio: functional assessment, sterile needle application and individual exercises. Rapid pain relief and better mobility.
pages.text: Dry needling therapy — step by step | DM Physio How dry needling works at DM Physio: functional assessment, sterile needle application and individual exercises. Rapid pain relief and better mobility. Dry needling therapy - step by step Dry Needling Therapy step by step Dry needling therapy is a modern technique for helping reduce muscle pain and releasing trigger points. By inserting very fine needles into specific muscle areas, a local therapeutic response is induced that relaxes the tissues, improves blood supply and supports recovery. Stage 1 (10 min.) Functional Assessment We start with a precise assessment of posture, range of motion and muscle imbalances to target the correct structures and maximize the effect of therapy. Individual assessment Identifying imbalances A clear recovery strategy Learn more Stage 2 (20–30 min.) Dry needling application Dry needling uses thin, sterile single-use needles that are inserted into tight muscle bands and trigger points. The needle produces a brief "local twitch response" that can help the muscle relax, can reduce sensitivity in the area and helps normalize muscle tone. Sterile disposable needles Pain relief Improvement of local circulation How it works The mechanism is twofold – local and neurophysiological. Locally, microcirculation is improved and spasm in the affected segment is reduced. Neurophysiologically, the pain signal is modulated and the muscle activation during movement can improve. Price: 35 euros. (30min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently asked questions Who is this procedure suitable for? Acute and chronic pain in the back, neck, shoulders, knees and other joints Recovery after sports injuries and operations Muscle imbalances and postural disorders (poor posture) Numbness, paresis and other neurological symptoms Headache from tension in neck and shoulders Decreased joint mobility and stiffness Injury prevention and fitness improvement How often should I come for therapy? We usually start with 1-2 sessions per week, then taper off as progress is made. We plan individually. Do I need to prepare? Wear comfortable clothing that allows free movement. Share previous injuries, surgeries or conditions. Pain conditions

pages.title: Ergon technique massage — step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Ergon technique (instrumental soft tissue mobilization) with precision stainless steel instruments to detect and treat soft tissue adhesions and fibrosis. Effective for acute and chronic musculoskeletal problems.
pages.text: Ergon technique massage — step by step | DM Physio Ergon technique (instrumental soft tissue mobilization) with precision stainless steel instruments to detect and treat soft tissue adhesions and fibrosis. Effective for acute and chronic musculoskeletal problems. Ergon technique massage - step by step Ergon massage step by step Ergon technique massage (Ergon technique) is an innovative therapeutic technique based on instrumental mobilization of soft tissues - mobilization of soft tissues with instruments. Specially designed stainless steel instruments are used to detect and treat adhesions and fibrosis that limit movement and cause pain. It is effective for both acute and chronic musculoskeletal problems. Procedure (60 min.) During the Ergon massage, the therapist applies targeted pressure and strokes using specialized tools. They allow more precise sensing and treatment of fibrotic changes and tissue tension than with hands alone. Key benefits include: Removal of adhesions and scars in soft tissues - improves elasticity. Pain reduction — especially in chronic conditions. Accelerated recovery - promotes natural recovery processes. Before the Massage (5–10 min.) Detailed Consultation and Evaluation Before starting the session, the therapist conducts a consultation and a functional assessment—motion analysis, palpation, and a discussion of medical history and symptoms. Accurate localization of the affected tissues is key to the effectiveness of Ergon therapy. Precise localization of problem areas An individual approach to your specific condition Optimizing therapy for steady progress Learn more about assessment Book an appointment Frequently asked questions about Ergon Massage What is Ergon therapy? Ergon therapy is an instrumental soft tissue mobilization technique with stainless steel instruments to detect and treat fibrous changes, adhesions and restrictions in muscles, fascia and tendons that cause pain and limit movement. Is Ergon therapy painful? Tolerable discomfort or mild pain is possible, especially in areas with adhesions or fibrosis. The pressure adapts to your tolerance — it's important to communicate how you feel. How many procedures are needed? It depends on the problem. For acute conditions, 1–3 sessions may be sufficient; in chronic cases, a longer course is needed. The plan is drawn up after the initial assessment. Are there any side effects? Slight redness, bruising, or tenderness in the treated areas is possible, which usually subsides within a few days. Good hydration and following directions are recommended. Pain conditions

pages.title: Functional Assessment - Step by Step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: A functional assessment shows which muscles and joints are working out of harmony to target the contributing factors of pain and imbalances. You get a personalized plan for faster recovery and more natural movement.
pages.text: Functional Assessment - Step by Step | DM Physio A functional assessment shows which muscles and joints are working out of harmony to target the contributing factors of pain and imbalances. You get a personalized plan for faster recovery and more natural movement. Functional assessment - step by step Functional Assessment step by step Functional assessment gives you an accurate picture of which muscles and joints are working out of harmony. Thus, we target the contributing factors of pain and imbalances and create a personalized plan that supports recovery, reduces the risk of re-injury and restores more natural movement. Stage (10–15 min.) System Overview and Plan A functional assessment is a systematic examination in which the therapist considers the body as a whole. After a brief discussion of pain, tingling and the contexts in which they occur, follows: Visual and kinetic analysis of posture and basic movements (bending, turning, walking) Palpation and specific tests for joint mobility and fascial flexibility Identifying chronically tight/shortened and overstretched/weakened muscles Finally, we prepare a personalized plan that includes manual therapy for "relaxation" and restructuring, targeted exercises to restore muscle length and strength, and supportive methods such as kinesio taping — to restore better balance and reduce the risk of recurrence. Free of charge Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently asked questions How does the therapist know I have an imbalance? The therapist looks for asymmetries — e.g. higher shoulder, tilted head, externally rotated feet when standing and walking. They show which muscles are chronically shortened and which are overstretched and weakened. How is imbalance assessed? Through functional tests of joint mobility and pain monitoring with exercise. Overworked muscles are often painful to palpation or active work. Is manual therapy painful? It is applied according to individual sensitivity — within comfortable limits so as not to cause protective spasm. The goal is effective and gentle therapy. Pain conditions

pages.title: Kinesio taping — step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Kinesio taping with elastic bands to reduce pain and swelling, support muscles and joints and improve recovery. Functional assessment and professional application.
pages.text: Kinesio taping — step by step | DM Physio Kinesio taping with elastic bands to reduce pain and swelling, support muscles and joints and improve recovery. Functional assessment and professional application. Kinesio taping — step by step Kinesio taping step by step Kinesio taping uses elastic bands that mimic the elasticity of the skin to support natural recovery processes: reduce pain and swelling, improve lymphatic drainage, and support muscles and joints—without restricting movement. The method is widely used in sports and physiotherapy. Stage 1 (10–15 min.) Kinesio taping application The tape is generally hypoallergenic, waterproof and can be worn for several days — while showering, exercising or sleeping. The application is performed by a trained specialist who determines the correct technique and direction for the desired effect. Application Objectives: Pain reduction by slightly lifting the skin and reducing the pressure on the pain receptors Reduction of swelling and bruising through facilitated lymphatic drainage and blood circulation Support of muscles and joints without restriction of movement Before Application (10–15 min.) Functional Assessment Prior to placement, a detailed functional assessment is performed to determine the exact muscles, joints or areas that need support and to select the most appropriate taping technique. Individual assessment Identifying problem areas Choosing the optimal taping technique Learn more Price: 15 euros. (20min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently Asked Questions About Kinesio Taping What does kinesio taping help? It reduces pain and swelling, improves lymphatic drainage and circulation, supports muscles and joints and improves proprioception. Does the tape restrict movement? No. The tape mimics the elasticity of the skin and allow complete freedom of movement. How long can the tape be worn and can I shower with it? The tape is water-resistant and usually worn for several days — you can shower, exercise and sleep with them. Is it safe for sensitive skin? They are generally hypoallergenic, but for very sensitive skin it is good to do a test on a small area. Are there any contraindications? Yes — do not apply to open wounds, active infections, fresh scars/burns, or allergies to adhesives. In special cases - after consultation. Can it replace therapy? The best long-term results usually come when taping is combined with exercises and appropriate therapeutic techniques. Pain conditions

pages.title: Manual therapy - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Manual therapy to relax soft tissues, improve blood supply and restore gliding between fascia and muscle. Sparing techniques: myofascial release, trigger points, longitudinal/transverse tissue techniques and postisometric relaxation.
pages.text: Manual therapy - step by step | DM Physio Manual therapy to relax soft tissues, improve blood supply and restore gliding between fascia and muscle. Sparing techniques: myofascial release, trigger points, longitudinal/transverse tissue techniques and postisometric relaxation. Manual therapy - step by step Manual therapy Manual therapy is targeted soft tissue work to reduce tension, improve local blood supply and restore smooth gliding between fascia and muscle. Specific grips are used according to tissue sensitivity and your goals to achieve easier movement and sustained symptom relief. (30–60 min.) The session takes place at a relaxed pace and combines myofascial release (to relax stuck layers), trigger point work with dosed hold, longitudinal and transverse tissue techniques to reduce sensitive areas, as well as gentle techniques to normalize muscle tone (eg postisometric relaxation and fascial stretches). Through rhythm, breathing and a gradual increase in tolerance, the tissues regain elasticity and coordination, resulting in a more efficient gait, less pulling in vulnerable areas and better control in everyday movements. Price: 25-50 euros (30-60 min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently asked questions Is manual therapy painful? Techniques are performed in a range that your body feels comfortable with. The goal is to have no pain—if the pressure is excessive, the muscles respond with spasm. Therefore, the load is adjusted so that it is effective but tolerable. Does it apply to cracked joints? No. This procedure is without manipulations and mobilizations . We work on soft tissue—muscles and fascia—to reduce tension and improve mobility in a gentle way. What results should I expect? Most often — reduction in pain and stiffness, easier movement and better posture. The effect is enhanced when manual therapy is combined with targeted exercises to activate and balance the muscles. Frequent pain conditions

pages.title: Relaxing massage - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Relaxing massage to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve sleep and general well-being. Gentle, smooth and rhythmic movements for deep relaxation.
pages.text: Relaxing massage - step by step | DM Physio Relaxing massage to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve sleep and general well-being. Gentle, smooth and rhythmic movements for deep relaxation. Relaxing massage - step by step Relaxing Massage step by step Relaxation massage is a gentle and soothing therapy that aims to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve overall well-being. Unlike remedial or deep tissue massage, the focus is on a sense of calm and harmony through smooth and rhythmic movements. Procedure (60 min.) During the relaxing massage, we use stroking, rubbing, kneading and light tapping aimed at the superficial layers of the muscles. The goal is to activate the parasympathetic nervous system for deep relaxation and stress reduction. Main benefits: Reducing stress and anxiety - stimulates the release of endorphins. Improving sleep — deeper and more restful sleep. Better blood circulation — better supply of tissues with oxygen. Indirect immune support — by reducing stress hormones. Relaxing massage is a great way to take care of yourself, recharge your energy and improve mental and physical well-being. Price: 50 euros. (60min) Book an appointment Relaxation Massage Frequently Asked Questions Who is a relaxing massage suitable for? For almost anyone looking for relief from stress, tension and fatigue. Suitable for people with a sedentary daily life, light seizures or for "a little time for yourself". How often can I have a relaxing massage? As needed and budgeted — often once a week or every two weeks as part of a stress management routine. Are there any side effects? Usually not. You may feel a little sleepy/flabby after the session - this is normal. Drink water after the massage. What should I expect during a relaxing massage? A quiet, peaceful setting and gentle, smooth movements. You will be covered with a towel; only the areas being worked on are detected. The goal is maximum comfort and relaxation. Pain conditions

pages.title: Relaxing massage - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Relaxing massage to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve sleep and general well-being. Gentle, smooth and rhythmic movements for deep relaxation.
pages.text: Relaxing massage - step by step | DM Physio Relaxing massage to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve sleep and general well-being. Gentle, smooth and rhythmic movements for deep relaxation. Relaxing massage - step by step Relaxing Massage step by step Relaxation massage is a gentle and soothing therapy that aims to reduce stress, relax tense muscles and improve overall well-being. Unlike remedial or deep tissue massage, the focus is on creating a sense of calm and harmony through smooth and rhythmic movements. Procedure (90 min.) During the relaxation massage, techniques such as stroking, rubbing, kneading and tapping are used, targeting the surface layers of the muscles. The goal is to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system for deep relaxation and stress reduction. Benefits include: Reducing stress and anxiety - stimulates the production of endorphins. Improving sleep — deeper and more restful sleep. Improvement of blood circulation — better supply of tissues with oxygen. Indirect immune support — by lowering stress hormones. Relaxing massage is a great way to take care of yourself, recharge your energy and improve your overall mental and physical health. Book an appointment Relaxation Massage Frequently Asked Questions Who is a relaxing massage suitable for? It is suitable for almost anyone looking for relief from stress, tension and fatigue. Ideal for people with long periods of sitting, light cramps or "me time". How often can I have a relaxing massage? As needed and budgeted — often once a week or every two weeks, as part of a stress management routine. Are there any side effects? It is usually safe and rarely has side effects. You may feel a little sleepy or lethargic after the massage. Drink water after the session. What should I expect during a relaxing massage? A quiet, peaceful setting and gentle, smooth movements. You will be covered with a towel; only the areas being worked on are detected, with comfort and relaxation as a priority. Pain conditions

pages.title: Specialized exercises - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Personalized, specialized exercises based on a functional assessment — to correct imbalances, improve range of motion, address contributing factors for pain, and prevent injuries.
pages.text: Specialized exercises - step by step | DM Physio Personalized, specialized exercises based on a functional assessment — to correct imbalances, improve range of motion, address contributing factors for pain, and prevent injuries. Specialized exercises - step by step Specialized Exercises step by step Specialized exercises are an important part of recovery and long-term movement control. They are selected according to the functional assessment and adapted to the specific weaknesses, limitations and goals of the person. The goal is not just temporary relief, but better control, freer movement, and reduction of factors that can maintain pain. Procedure (30 min) Our specialized exercise programs are built around your assessment and goals. They combine mobility, strength, stability and coordination so movement becomes clearer, safer and easier to repeat. Often the body has repeated the same movement pattern for a long time. Some muscles take too much load, others do too little, and the result can be irritation, stiffness or recurring soreness. With the exercises, we build the opposite habit: slower, controlled movements that teach the body a more comfortable and efficient pattern. Each exercise is performed under careful control - the therapist demonstrates the technique, corrects muscle activation and monitors correct execution. This helps improve muscle activation, strengthen weaker groups and make the new pattern easier to use outside the clinic. The home program provides constant practice that helps the new movements gradually be transferred into everyday life. Before the program Functional Assessment (10-15 min.) Before we begin the specialized exercises, we do a brief functional assessment: we check ranges of motion, stability, control and coordination in key segments. Based on the results, we choose the right exercises, set the appropriate load (sets/reps/tempo) and determine the home program and progression. Mobility and stability tests Identifying priority muscle groups Personalized exercises and progression Learn more Price: 30 euros. (One time price) Book an appointment Frequently Asked Questions about Specialized Exercises: How are the exercises selected? Exercises are selected based on the functional assessment and are aimed at specific weaknesses, imbalances and inefficient movement patterns. The progression (sets, reps, pace, difficulty) adapts to your response and goals. How often should I train and how long does the session last? Usually 3–5 times a week, according to the plan. One session is about 30 minutes, with the load increasing gradually to be effective and tolerable. Is special equipment needed at home? It is not mandatory. Most exercises use body weight and basic equipment (elastic band, mini band, chair/mat). If you have additional equipment, the plan may include it. What should I do if I feel sore after a workout? Distinguish between normal muscle fatigue and sharp, stabbing pain. In case of severe pain, stop the exercise and notify the therapist - we will adjust the technique, volume or intensity and, if necessary, modify the program. Pain conditions

pages.title: Sports massage - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Sports massage for active sportsmen and athletes: supports recovery, prevents injuries and improves performance through deep, targeted techniques.
pages.text: Sports massage - step by step | DM Physio Sports massage for active sportsmen and athletes: supports recovery, prevents injuries and improves performance through deep, targeted techniques. Sports massage - step by step Sports Massage step by step Sports massage is a specialized therapy for active sportsmen and athletes. It supports post-workout recovery, prevents injury and improves athletic performance through deeper, targeted techniques to specific muscle groups and connective tissues. Procedure (60 min.) During sports massage, deep rubbing, kneading, friction and stretching are applied to reach the deeper layers of muscles and fascia. The focus is on increasing circulation, breaking down adhesions and knots, and improving tissue elasticity. Benefits include: Accelerated muscle recovery — less soreness and fatigue after workouts. Injury prevention — more flexibility and lower risk of sprains. Better performance — optimizes muscle function and range of motion. Sports massage is a key part of any athlete's regimen — it keeps the body in optimal shape and supports long-term athletic development. Before the Massage (5–10 min.) Brief Consultation and Assessment Before the massage begins, we discuss sports loads, symptoms, pain areas and medical history. So we target the groups with the greatest need and adapt the efficiency and safety techniques according to your goals. Precise focusing on sports-loaded areas Individual plan according to your goals Optimal results and quality recovery Learn more about assessment Book an appointment Frequently asked questions about Sports Massage Who is sports massage suitable for? For athletes and active sportsmen, as well as for people with muscle tension/fatigue after physical activity. It is suitable before events for preparation and after them for recovery. How often should I get a sports massage? It depends on the load. Often — once a week or every two weeks for active sportsmen; with less frequent activity — when needed or around specific events. Is sports massage painful? It is more intense than the relaxing one and there may be some tolerable discomfort. The goal is not pain, but deep work. Report severe pain to adjust pressure. What should I expect during a sports massage? Targeted work on the stressed muscles, stronger pressure, friction and stretching. Momentary relief or mild muscle soreness the next day is possible. Drink water after the session. Pain conditions

pages.title: Therapeutic massage - step by step | DM Physio
pages.excerpt: Therapeutic massage to relieve pain, reduce muscle tension and improve blood circulation. Personalized techniques: deep tissue massage, myofascial release, trigger points and stretching.
pages.text: Therapeutic massage - step by step | DM Physio Therapeutic massage to relieve pain, reduce muscle tension and improve blood circulation. Personalized techniques: deep tissue massage, myofascial release, trigger points and stretching. Therapeutic massage - step by step Therapeutic Massage step by step Therapeutic massage is a targeted therapeutic technique designed to relieve pain, reduce muscle tension, improve circulation, and aid tissue recovery after injury or overuse. It differs from relaxation massage in its focus on specific problem areas and the use of deeper techniques to affect muscles and connective tissue. Stage Procedure (60 min.) Therapeutic massage can be effective for both acute and chronic conditions, and is often combined with physiotherapy or other physical therapy methods to achieve steady progress. It's a personalized approach to your condition. The therapist uses a variety of techniques such as deep tissue massage, myofascial release, trigger point work and therapeutic stretching to address specific muscle dysfunctions, spasms and pain conditions. The goal is not just temporary relief, but long-lasting improvement through: Pain relief — reduces tension in muscles and nerve structures Improving blood circulation — better tissue trophicity and clearance of metabolites Speeding up recovery — especially for sports injuries and overuse Price: 50 euros. (60min) Book an appointment --> Book an appointment Frequently asked questions about Therapeutic Massage What is the difference between therapeutic and relaxing massage? Relaxation massage is aimed at general relaxation and stress reduction. Therapeutic massage uses deeper and specific techniques on muscles, fascia and connective tissue to address pain, limited mobility and other musculoskeletal problems. How often is it necessary to have a therapeutic massage? Frequency depends on your condition, severity of symptoms, and goals. For acute conditions, more frequent sessions (2-3/week) may be needed, while for chronic or maintenance conditions, once every 1-2 weeks. The therapist prepares an individual plan. Is therapeutic massage painful? Moderate "good" discomfort is possible when treating tense areas, but the massage should not be excruciatingly painful. Pressure and technique adapt to your tolerance. Are there any contraindications for therapeutic massage? Yes. For acute infections, high fever, varicose veins (in the affected area), certain skin diseases, thrombosis and certain heart problems, it should be used with caution or avoided. Specific techniques are used during pregnancy. Always inform the therapist about health conditions. Pain conditions
